content.full_text:
CITY OF Glendale CALIFORNIA
613 E. Broadway, Suite 220, Glendale, CA 91206—4394 (818) 956-2080

Office of the
CITY ATTORNEY

August 3, 1990

Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. Box 8067
Sacramento, CA 95804

Re: Request for Formal Written Advice
Our File: 500-1

Gentlemen:

As the City Attorney for the City of Glendale, I am hereby requesting formal written advice pursuant to Government Code Section 83114(b) and section 18329 of the Fair Political Practices Commission regulations.

I have been expressly authorized by the Honorable Richard Jutras, Council Member and Member of the Redevelopment Agency for the City of Glendale, to make this request relating to his responsibilities under the Fair Political Practices Act, as well as Health and Safety Code Section 33130.

BACKGROUND INFORMATION

The Honorable Richard Jutras was elected by the people of the City of Glendale to the position of Council Member on April 4, 1989. As a member of the City Council of the City of Glendale, Mr. Jutras sits with other members as a member of the Glendale Redevelopment Agency, a body politic, organized and existing pursuant to Health and Safety Code Section 33020 et seq.

The Redevelopment Agency of the City of Glendale has created a project area in and around the Central Business District of the City of Glendale. Pursuant thereto, the Redevelopment Agency approves plans or policies for the redevelopment of the project area and is called upon to vote on such projects from time to time.

Recently, Agency Member Jutras was offered a business proposition to participate as a 20% shareholder in a corporate venture to lease real property within the redevelopment area to operate a proposed model railroad retail establishment. The property to be leased is within the redevelopment project area and has already been the subject of a project which was approved prior to Mr. Jutras' election to the City Council and subsequent appointment to the Glendale Redevelopment Agency.

Fair Political Practices Commission
August 3, 1990
Page Two


The property which would be subject to the lease is now under private
ownership. Additionally, the model railroad establishment will be
operated on a day-to-day basis by a non-owner/manager. As a result,
Mr. Jutras' involvement will be strictly as a passive investor.

Our office has prepared a legal opinion in which an analysis of the
various applicable code sections, including Health & Safety Code
Section 33130 and Government Code Sections 87000 et seq. has been
made. We have opined that Agency Member Jutras cannot acquire the
interest in real property which has been offered pursuant to 33130(c)
of the Health & Safety Code in that the operation of a model railroad
establishment is not used in the pursuit of the principal business
occupation or profession of Mr. Jutras. The additional question has
been posed as to whether such interest could be taken by Mr. Jutras'
spouse or adult child. Our review of Health & Safety Code Section
33130 seems to restrict only the Agency officer from acquiring any
interest in real property and requires that an officer who owns or has
any direct or indirect financial interest in property must disclose
same to the Agency.

REQUEST FOR WRITTEN ADVICE

Your advice is requested with respect to the following issues:

     1. Under Health & Safety Code Section 33130, can Agency Member
Jutras' adult spouse or child acquire a 20% shareholder's interest in
property within the redevelopment area without subjecting himself to a
potential claim of misconduct in office under Section 33130(a).

     2. Under the materiality rules by the FPPC, assuming that Agency
Member Jutras' spouse or adult child could acquire an interest in the
aforesaid property pursuant to Health & Safety Code § 33130, what
guidelines would you formulate for us to utilize in determining the
nature and extent of allowable participation by Agency Member Jutras
in voting upon future projects which might come before the Agency for
determination.

If you have any questions or if you require additional information
regarding this request, please do not hesitate to contact me. Because
the offer has recently been made to Mr. Jutras and is of short
duration and, additionally, because the redevelopment project area
activity is somewhat dynamic, we would sincerely appreciate your most
expeditious attention to this request.

Thank you again for your courtesy and professionalism.

Very truly yours,

Scott H. Howard
City Attorney

a:fppc.ltr

sections.question_synthetic: Whether a City Council member and Redevelopment Agency member may acquire, through his spouse or adult child, a shareholding interest in a business operating within the redevelopment project area, and if so, what voting restrictions or disclosure requirements would apply to the official.
sections.parsing_notes: Format: old; LLM: This is an incoming request from City Attorney to FPPC, not an FPPC response letter. No conclusion section present. Document is dated August 3, 1990, and references Gov't Code Sections 83114(b) and 18329 for formal advice request authority. Background section explains the conflict of interest concern regarding property in the redevelopment area.

Under the materiality rules by the FPPC, assuming that Agency Member Jutras' spouse or adult child could acquire an interest in the aforesaid property pursuant to Health & Safety Code S 33130, what guidelines would you formulate for us to utilize in determining the nature and extent of allowable participation by Agency Member Jutras in voting upon future projects which might come before the Agency for determination.

embedding.summary: City of Glendale requests formal written FPPC advice regarding whether Redevelopment Agency Member Richard Jutras may indirectly acquire a shareholding interest in a model railroad business within the redevelopment project area through his spouse or adult child, and what voting guidelines would apply.
